description: A wide (1200x675) designed holiday greeting card from DHS marking the Islamic festival of Eid al-Adha. The composition uses a green gradient background (lighter center fading to darker edges). On the left is a flat silhouette illustration of a mosque archway/window in slightly darker green with a thin orange-gold outline, containing a large pale-green crescent moon; smaller mosque-dome silhouettes with tiny crescents sit along the bottom. On the upper right, a row of six ornate hanging lanterns in dark-green silhouette dangle from the top edge. Centered-right, elegant white script reads 'Eid al-Adha' above the spaced-out serif word 'MUBARAK' flanked by two small stars. Bottom-right corner shows the circular blue-and-white U.S. Department of Homeland Security seal. Style: tasteful, decorative seasonal e-card using traditional Islamic visual motifs (crescent, lanterns, mosque silhouettes) in a monochrome-green palette with calligraphic typography. Intent: inclusive holiday outreach extending good wishes to Muslim communities/employees (Biden-era DHS festive messaging).
